title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: The private journals of a Roman emperor — written for no one but himself. Every page is a lesson in stoicism, discipline, and the good life.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
description: 13 short chapters that have shaped military strategy, business thinking, and competitive sport for 2,500 years. Deceptively simple, endlessly useful.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: The original sci-fi novel — written by a 18-year-old. Not the monster story you think you know. It's about ambition, responsibility, and what makes us human.

title: The Time Machine
author: H.G. Wells
description: A scientist travels to the year 802,701 and finds humanity split into two species. An early masterwork of social commentary disguised as adventure.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: The most entertaining novel about marriage, money, and social class ever written. Wickedly funny, surprisingly modern. Elizabeth Bennet is unforgettable.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: 12 of Holmes's greatest cases — The Speckled Band, A Scandal in Bohemia, and more. Read them in order or pick any you like.

title: The Metamorphosis
author: Franz Kafka
description: A man wakes up as a giant insect. In 60 pages, Kafka says more about alienation, family duty, and modern anxiety than most novels do in 500.

title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Lewis Carroll
description: The original mind-bending fantasy — read as a child, understood as an adult. Full of logic paradoxes, wordplay, and hidden social satire.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
description: Nietzsche's most famous work — a poetic philosophical novel about the Übermensch, eternal recurrence, and the death of God. Dense but rewarding.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: Captain Ahab's obsessive hunt for the white whale. One of the greatest novels ever written — and one of the most misunderstood. Start Chapter 1, trust the journey.

title: The Republic
author: Plato
description: Socrates debates justice, the ideal city, and the nature of the soul. The Allegory of the Cave alone is worth the read. Foundations of Western thought.

title: The Jungle Book
author: Rudyard Kipling
description: Mowgli's story — but the original is richer, stranger, and deeper than any adaptation. Includes Rikki-Tikki-Tavi and other unforgettable tales.

title: On the Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
description: The book that changed everything — Darwin explains natural selection with clarity and care. Surprisingly readable. The most important scientific book ever written.

title: The War of the Worlds
author: H.G. Wells
description: Martians invade Victorian England. The original alien invasion story — still tense, still relevant, still a masterpiece of science fiction.

title: Think and Grow Rich
author: Napoleon Hill
description: Synthesised from interviews with 500 of the most successful people of the early 20th century — Carnegie, Edison, Ford. The philosophy of personal achievement.
